channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: The Apple Sports app expands to 90 new markets ahead of the World Cup
item.description: Apple is targeting World Cup fans ahead of this summer's tournament.

item.title: Here are our favorite Memorial Day tech deals so far
item.description: Memorial Day marks the unofficial start of summer, and the holiday’s sales include plenty of deals on gadgets that can help you make the most of the season. If your plans involve beach days, pool parties, or backyard barbecues, now’s a great time to pick up a portable Bluetooth speaker, as many of our favorite […]

item.title: Google debuts AI-powered tools to optimize scientific research workflows
item.description: Three new features will be available under the Gemini for Science collection.

item.title: Everything announced at the Google I/O 2026 keynote: AI, more AI, and smart glasses
item.description: Everything announced at Google I/O 2026 keynote

item.title: Apples AI-powered accessibility upgrades include eye-controlled wheelchairs
item.description: Apple is adding Apple Intelligence features to its popular accessibility tools, including Voice Over, live captioning, and even Apple Vision Pro.

item.title: Democrats preview how they’d go after the Ticketmaster settlement if they regain power
item.description: A handful of Democrats called an unofficial hearing on Capitol Hill Monday to slam the Department of Justice's "trivial" and "pathetic" settlement with Live Nation-Ticketmaster, previewing how they might go after Trump administration antitrust deals if they win back congressional power in November. While the DOJ settlement with Live Nation, which came one week into […]...

item.title: Elon Musk said Sam Altman “stole” a non-profit — but the trial showed he had similar aims
item.description: “By the end of this week, you and Sam will be the most hated men in America.”

item.title: Google Pics is a new Workspace tool that sounds like a big Canva competitor
item.description: At Google I/O 2026, the company debuted the new Workspace program, Google Pics, which felt quite familiar. So is the AI-powered Google Pics the new Canva?

item.title: Want to get promoted at work? New study says learning new AI skills is the key to a pay rise
item.description: Companies are paying AI-skilled workers more than their peers, but they’re also offering upskilling support, so what’s to lose?

item.title: Everything Announced at Google I/O 2026: Gemini, Search, Smart Glasses
item.description: Google is sprucing up its Gemini models, revamping search, and enabling AI agents in everything. There are also some spiffy new smart glasses coming this fall.

item.title: How to watch The Super Mario Galaxy Movie online and from anywhere
item.description: Wahoo! It's time to find the best way to rent or buy The Super Mario Galaxy Movie online as the animated sequel hits the digital market

item.title: Fortnite is returning to the App Store globally, a year after its US comeback
item.description: Fortnite is returning to the App Store everywhere (except Australia).

item.title: Ugreen’s new soccer ball-shaped tracker has up to 7 years of battery life
item.description: Ugreen has announced a new Apple Find My-compatible tracker with a novel design that limits where you can hide it, but greatly benefits battery life. Inspired by "major football tournaments" the company doesn't call out by name, the Ugreen FineTrack 2 is shaped like a soccer ball and is designed to be used hanging off […]

item.title: Claude agents can finally connect to enterprise APIs without leaking credentials
item.description: The reason enterprises have been slow to connect AI agents to internal APIs and databases isn't the models — it's the credentials. In most production deployments, the agent carries authentication tokens with it as it executes tool calls, which means a compromised or misbehaving agent takes the keys with it.Anthropic is addressing that problem with two new capabilities for Claude Managed Agents: self-hosted sandboxes, which let teams run tool execution inside their own infrastructure perimeter, and MCP tunnels, which connect agents to private MCP servers without exposing credentials in the agen...

item.title: Google announces Wear OS 7
item.description: The OS update integrates Gemini Intelligence and adds a new widget system.

item.title: Minnesota passes prediction markets ban
item.description: The CFTC has already sued to prevent the Minnesota's prediction markets ban.

item.title: Les nouveaux Surface Pro 12 et Laptop 8 débarquent, mais réservés aux pros pour le moment
item.description: Microsoft renouvelle ses Surface Pro et Surface Laptop avec les puces Intel Panther Lake. Sur le papier c'est solide. Sur l'étiquette, beaucoup moins : le ticket d'entrée frôle les 2 000 dollars.

item.title: Google wants to give you an AI-driven Daily Brief
item.description: Google's announcements at Google I/O 2026 included a Daily Brief for paid Google AI subscribers.

item.title: China unveils a CPU-only supercomputer capable of 1.54 exaflops — LineShine LX2 packs a frankly ridiculous 2.4 million Armv9 cores from Huawei
item.description: China developed a CPU-only exaflop supercomputer using millions of Arm cores to reduce dependence on restricted foreign GPUs.

item.title: YouTube: 2 new announcements from Google I/O
item.description: At its annual developer conference, Google announced it's bringing YouTube to AI Search.

item.title: Gemini 3.5 Flash, Gemini Omni, Spark et lunettes Android XR : toutes les annonces de la Google I/O 2026
item.description: Voici le résumé complet des annonces de la Google I/O 2026 avec de très nombreuses nouveautés pour Gemini et des annonces Android XR.

item.title: Google takes a page out of Meta’s book, announces new audio-powered smart glasses
item.description: Google is calling the new devices "audio glasses," in that users will be able to issue verbal commands to them and get things done via its ecosystem of apps and services, including Gemini.

item.title: Project Aura : les lunettes XR de Google et XREAL passent au concret
item.description: Annoncées il y a un an, les lunettes Android XR de XREAL passent enfin du concept au hardware réel à Google I/O 2026. Avec une promesse forte, et un point de friction qui divise déjà.

item.title: Amazon launches a huge Garmin sale for Memorial Day — here are the 8 best smartwatches we've tested and recommend from $249.99
item.description: Garmin smartwatches are on sale right now in the Memorial Day sales, so I've rounded up the 8 best deals on the wearables we've tested and rate highly here at TechRadar.

item.title: Meta Employees Are Scrambling to Use Up Benefits Ahead of Layoffs
item.description: On the eve of about 8,000 jobs being cut, employees are cashing in on headphone stipends and other perks while they still can.

item.title: Your smart home devices are a major privacy risk – here’s how to secure them
item.description: Your smart home devices could be silently sharing your daily routine with ISPs and third parties. Here is how to lock down your network and reclaim your digital privacy using a router VPN.

item.title: Google Gemini's Verify AI might finally solve my online image trust issues — especially with support from Nvidia and OpenAI
item.description: Google I/O 2026 has showcased a possible solution to my online image trust issues: Verify AI.

item.title: YouTube is rolling out its ‘industry-first’ likeness detection tool to all channel owners, and not just big content creators — but you’ll need to share your government-issued ID if you want the added layer of protection
item.description: YouTube's likeness detection tool is rolling out to all channel owners, but you need to submit a government-issued ID to get started.

item.title: Google Search is getting its biggest upgrade in decades — here are the 5 best new features
item.description: At Google I/O 2026, the company rolled out plenty of updates, but search got a particularly big highlight with AI integrated into pretty much every part of it.

item.title: Is your business not showing up in AI overviews? Trustpilot thinks it could be because you're not doing this one thing
item.description: Embracing customer reviews boosts visibility considerably, showing how engaging with customers where they are is even more important.

item.title: Google’s AI strategy is finally coming into focus
item.description: The search giant no longer just “organizes the world’s information.” With Gemini, it wants AI to reason over that information and increasingly operate on the user’s behalf. In a major salvo in the AI race, Google announced on Tuesday a slew of new and updated products at its I/O developer conference. These ranged from tools that deploy personal AI agents to code generators to search tools to a new “world model” for generating physically accurate video....
